name: Malaysian Peanut Sauce
recipeIngredient: Coconut milk or water to thin to desired consistency
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a small wok or pan over moderate heat.
2. Place garlic and onion flakes in a fine wire strainer and lower into the oil. Cook until golden.
3. Drain on paper towels. Fry whole chiles for about 40 seconds, then remove and drain on paper towels.
4. Let cool. Discard chile seeds.
5. Fry shrimp paste in remaining oil, crushing with the back of a spoon.
6. Add lime juice and soy sauce. Remove from heat.
7. Add peanut butter and sugar. Stir to blend well.
8. Cool. Crumble garlic flakes, onion flakes, and chiles into small pieces and add to mixture.
9. Combine well. Serve at room temperature.
10. Advance preparation: Prepare up to 1 week in advance. Store in a tightly covered glass container in the refrigerator. If sauce is too thick, add coconut milk or water to reach desired consistency.
